question_text: A hydraulic system has a small piston with area 0.5 m² and a large piston with area 10 m². A force of 500 N is applied to the small piston. Calculate the force exerted by the large piston, assuming no energy is lost.
explanation: In a hydraulic system, pressure is transmitted equally through the fluid. Using P = F/A, the pressure from the small piston equals the pressure at the large piston. Rearranging: F₂ = F₁ × (A₂/A₁) = 500 × (10/0.5) = 10,000 N. This shows how hydraulic systems can multiply force.
